subject: Sports
title: The Boycott Games
description: A 60-second lesson on The Boycott Games.

hook.text: The Cold War turned the Olympics into a political battleground. Athletes paid the price.

buildup.text: In 1980, the US led a 65-nation boycott of the Moscow Games to protest the Soviet invasion of Afghanistan.

discovery.text: In retaliation, the Soviet Union and 13 allies boycotted the 1984 Los Angeles Games.

twist.text: Hundreds of athletes lost their only chance at Olympic glory—not from losing, but from politics.

climax.text: The boycotts weakened both Games. The IOC worked to prevent political interference ever since.

punchline.text: Politicians boycotted. Athletes lost dreams they'd never get back.

quiz.question: Why did the US boycott the 1980 Moscow Olympics?
options: To protest the Soviet invasion of Afghanistan
